pom.xml.general_idea: Set the Maven compiler release target to Java 21 to ensure bytecode compatibility with the new JDK version.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project would compile against the default JDK version (likely 17 or lower), failing to utilize Java 21 features or potentially causing runtime issues if Java 21-specific APIs are used.

raspberrypi-boot-bmp085/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the Spring Boot parent version to 3.3.13 to align with Java 21 and modern Spring ecosystem standards.
raspberrypi-boot-bmp085/pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.x does not support Java 21 as a baseline; using it with Java 21 may cause runtime failures or lack of support for newer language features.

raspberrypi-boot-bmp085/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property to 21 to target the new JDK version.
raspberrypi-boot-bmp085/pom.xml.why_exists: The project must compile and run against Java 21; keeping it at 17 would result in incorrect bytecode or missing language features.

raspberrypi-boot-bmp085/pom.xml.general_idea: Remove the explicit version of jackson-databind to rely on the version managed by the Spring Boot parent BOM.
raspberrypi-boot-bmp085/pom.xml.why_exists: Explicitly pinning a version that is incompatible with Spring Boot 3.x dependencies can lead to classpath conflicts or runtime errors.

raspberrypi-boot-gdata/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the Spring Boot parent version to 3.3.13 to align with Java 21 support and modern framework features.
raspberrypi-boot-gdata/pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.x does not support Java 21 as a baseline; using it with Java 21 may cause runtime incompatibilities or lack of support for newer language features.

raspberrypi-boot-gdata/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property to 21 to target the new JDK baseline.
raspberrypi-boot-gdata/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the build would target Java 17, failing to utilize Java 21 features or potentially causing mismatches with dependencies requiring Java 21.

raspberrypi-boot-pushover/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the Spring Boot parent version to 3.3.13 to align with Java 21 support and modern Spring ecosystem standards.
raspberrypi-boot-pushover/pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.x does not support Java 21 as a baseline; using it with Java 21 may cause runtime failures or lack of support for newer language features.

raspberrypi-boot-pushover/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property to 21 to target the new JDK baseline.
raspberrypi-boot-pushover/pom.xml.why_exists: The project must compile and run against Java 21; keeping it at 17 would result in incorrect compiler flags and potential incompatibilities with Java 21-specific APIs or behaviors.

raspberrypi-boot-server/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the Spring Boot parent version to 3.3.13 to align with the Java 21 migration.
raspberrypi-boot-server/pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.x does not support Java 21 as a baseline; using it with Java 21 causes compilation errors or runtime incompatibilities due to removed APIs and stricter bytecode requirements.

raspberrypi-boot-server/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property to 21 to target the new JDK.
raspberrypi-boot-server/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the build would target Java 17 bytecode, failing to utilize Java 21 features or potentially causing mismatches if the runtime is strictly Java 21.
